title: These Stocks Were Left Behind In November's Historic Rally
text: Energy was the sole S&P 500 sector to decline this month as the index marched toward its best month since July 2022.

title: Why Are Oil Stocks Down Today?
text: Oil stocks are down today as investors prepare for the OPEC+ meeting this week that should provide details of what to expect for oil production in 2024. OPEC+ and Russia are expected to meet and discuss production plans for countries producing oil in Africa.

title: 3 Integrated US Energy Stocks Set to Escape Industry Weakness
text: Extreme volatility in oil and gas prices and high input prices in refining activities are making the outlook for the Zacks Oil & Gas US Integrated industry gloomy. ConocoPhillips (COP), Occidental (OXY) and Marathon Oil (MRO) are likely to survive the business challenges.

title: Here's Why Hold Strategy is Apt for ConocoPhillips (COP) Now
text: ConocoPhillips (COP) to gain from the current highly favorable oil pricing scenario. This is likely to persist, creating a positive business environment for its exploration and production operations.

title: Wall Street Thinks These 5 Energy Dividend Stocks Will Double by 2030
text: The setup for the benchmark Oil indices of Brent Crude and West Texas Intermediate to soar over $100 was all in place.

title: ConocoPhillips' (COP) Willow Oil Project Wins Legal Battle
text: U.S. District Court judge greenlights ConocoPhillips' (COP) $7.5B Willow project, balancing energy needs and environmental scrutiny in Alaska's North Slope.

title: Energy looks to dominate markets with 3 oil and gas stocks
text: They say that you should take analyst projections and ratings with a grain of salt and that the actual language of the market is read through valuation ratios and price action. You could follow this logic, but as often does, it will probably lead you down a rabbit hole of ideas and wasted brain power.

title: Looking for More Income? These Great Dividend Stocks Just Gave Their Investors Another Big Raise.
text: ConocoPhillips just raised its payout by 14%, achieving its goal to provide above-average dividend growth. EOG Resources boosted its dividend by another 10% (and has delivered 21% compound annual dividend growth over the last quarter-century).

title: High Yield Stock Watchlist - November 2023
text: There are 22 stocks on my high yield watchlist for November 2023. The majority of the stocks on my watchlist are undervalued based on dividend yield theory. An equally weighted portfolio of these stocks underperformed the Vanguard High Yield Dividend ETF by about 1% during the month of October.

title: COP vs. WHD: Which Stock Should Value Investors Buy Now?
text: Investors with an interest in Oil and Gas - Integrated - United States stocks have likely encountered both ConocoPhillips (COP) and Cactus, Inc. (WHD). But which of these two stocks offers value investors a better bang for their buck right now?

title: With Giant Oil Mergers Upending the Energy Industry, Here Are 3 Investment Options to Consider Today
text: Lean into the trend with Exxon, which is big enough to keep consolidating the oil and gas industry.  Devon is already on the prowl for its next transformational deal.

title: Why These 7 Overlooked Value Stocks Could Be Your Golden Opportunity
text: With the major equity indices signaling the possibility of a slowdown – if not an outright downcycle – investors may be better served considering overlooked value stocks. At this moment, I like the idea of self-preservation, meaning that taking on hyped growth names may not be prudent.

title: ConocoPhillips profits take hit from lower prices as Duke Energy reports earnings beat
text: U.S. energy companies ConocoPhillips (NYSE:COP) and Duke Energy Corp (NYSE:DUK) reported mixed earnings on Thursday.

title: ConocoPhillips' Record Production, Favorable Oil Prices Fuel Earnings Beat
text: ConocoPhillips (COP) beat earnings predictions for the third quarter after revenue got a boost from increased production levels and rebounding oil prices.

title: ConocoPhillips (COP) Beats on Q3 Earnings, Hikes Dividend
text: Higher oil equivalent production volumes aid ConocoPhillips' (COP) earnings in Q3.

title: ConocoPhillips (COP) Q3 Earnings: Taking a Look at Key Metrics Versus Estimates
text: The headline numbers for ConocoPhillips (COP) give insight into how the company performed in the quarter ended September 2023, but it may be worthwhile to compare some of its key metrics to Wall Street estimates and the year-ago actuals.

title: ConocoPhillips profit and production top estimates as oil major raises quarterly dividend by 14%
text: ConocoPhillips' stock COP, -1.79% rose 0.9% in premarket trade Thursday, after the energy giant posted better-than-expected third-quarter profit and said it's raising its quarterly dividend by 14%. The company had net income of $2.8 billion, or $2.32 a share, for the quarter, down from $4.5 billion, or $3.55 a share, in the year-earlier period.

title: ConocoPhillips Reports Third-Quarter 2023 Results; Announces 14% Increase in Quarterly Ordinary Dividend
text: HOUSTON--(BUSINESS WIRE)--ConocoPhillips today reported third-quarter 2023 earnings of $2.8 billion, or $2.32 per share.

title: Why Earnings Season Could Be Great for ConocoPhillips (COP)
text: ConocoPhillips (COP) is seeing favorable earnings estimate revision activity and has a positive Zacks Earnings ESP heading into earnings season.

title: Up 15% In Last Six Months, Will ConocoPhillips Stock Continue To Grow Post Q3?
text: We expect ConocoPhillips stock to trade lower post Q3 with both revenue and earnings missing market expectations marginally.
